div.head: The British Prime Minister (Churchill) to President Roosevelt Copy transmitted to the Department by the British Embassy on October 1, 1943.
div.p: [427.]Number supplied from copy of telegram obtained from the Franklin D. Roosevelt Library, Hyde Park, N.Y. Your No. 363.Dated September 29, p. 378. I agree that we should make a joint announcement but would it not be a good chance of getting Uncle Joe in too? It is clear now, from correction of Molotov’s message,See footnote 5, p. 378. that he does accept the Italians as co-belligerents. It is true we may lose a few days in communicating with Moscow, but this delay seems relatively unimportant compared with value of Russian participation.

If you agree, would you put it to Stalin in the form that we wish an announcement of the kind made; will he join with us in making it, or would he prefer us to go ahead without him? Of course we should consider any drafting alterations he might wish to propose.

I myself would like to see several changes, and my immediately following telegram Infra. embodies these. If you see no objection to them would you, if you agree, approach Stalin and put text to him in this form.
